 {"id":"brenner_bot-0r50","title":"TASK: Implement Active Corpus Search in Sessions","description":"
## Search the Corpus From Within a Session

Users in a Brenner Loop session should be able to actively search the corpus, not just receive passive quote surfacing.

### Use Cases

1. **Research support**: "What did Brenner say about levels of explanation?"
2. **Quote attachment**: Find a quote and attach it to your hypothesis
3. **Example discovery**: "Show me examples of exclusion tests"
4. **Inspiration**: Browse relevant sections when stuck

### Integration Points

#### Search Panel in Session

\`\`\`
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Session: Social Media Hypothesis           [🔍 Search Corpus] │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

When clicked:

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Search Brenner Corpus                                    [×]  │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                 │
│  [What did Brenner say about testing hypotheses?        ] [🔍] │
│                                                                 │
│  Results:                                                       │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │ "The most important thing is to design experiments     │   │
│  │ that can give you a clean answer..."                   │   │
│  │                           — Transcript, p. 42           │   │
│  │ [View in Context] [Attach to Hypothesis] [Copy]         │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                 │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
\`\`\`

#### Attachment Feature

When user clicks "Attach to Hypothesis":
- Quote linked in hypothesis metadata
- Shown in Research Brief
- Builds citation list automatically

\`\`\`typescript
interface AttachedQuote {
  text: string;
  source: {
    document: 'transcript' | 'distillation' | 'quotebank';
    section: string;
    page?: number;
  };
  attachedTo: {
    hypothesisId: string;
    field: 'statement' | 'mechanism' | 'prediction' | 'general';
  };
  attachedAt: Date;
}
\`\`\`

#### Quick Search Shortcuts

From operator forms:
- "Find examples" button → searches for that operator type
- "What would Brenner say?" → contextual corpus search

### Search Modes

1. **Keyword**: Traditional text search
2. **Semantic**: "Find quotes about levels" (uses embeddings if available)
3. **Operator-specific**: "Show Level Split examples"
4. **Concept**: "Quotes about falsification"

### File Locations
\`\`\`
apps/web/src/components/brenner-loop/CorpusSearch.tsx
apps/web/src/components/brenner-loop/QuoteAttachment.tsx
apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/corpusSearch.ts
\`\`\`

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Search panel opens from session
- [ ] Results show with context
- [ ] Quotes can be attached to hypothesis
- [ ] Attached quotes appear in Research Brief
- [ ] Works with existing search infrastructure

### Depends On
- reew.1 (Session Dashboard Layout)
- Existing corpus search functionality

 {"id":"brenner_bot-0rs","title":"Docs: fix apps/web test run commands","description":"## Problem
Several vitest-based unit tests in `apps/web/src/lib/*.test.ts` have header comments suggesting running them with `bun test ...`.

## Why this matters
AGENTS.md is explicit: for the web app, tests must be run via `cd apps/web && bun run test` so Vitest + happy-dom are configured. Suggesting `bun test` causes confusing failures (e.g. "document is not defined") and makes local dev slower.

## Fix
Update the header comments to recommend:
- `cd apps/web && bun run test -- <file>`

## Acceptance
- No behavior changes; only comments.
- Grep for `Run with: bun test apps/web` returns nothing.

 {"id":"brenner_bot-0s7","title":"Legacy: Build excerpt builder component (superseded)","description":"# Task

Create the excerpt builder that allows users to select text from documents and build cited excerpt blocks.

# User Flow

1. User browses a document (e.g., transcript)
2. User selects text
3. Selection popover appears: 'Add to excerpt'
4. Selection added to 'Excerpt Basket' (sidebar or drawer)
5. User can add multiple selections
6. User composes final excerpt with ordering and context
7. Export as formatted markdown for session kickoff

# Excerpt Basket

- List of selections
- Each shows: text snippet, source doc, §-anchor
- Drag to reorder
- Delete button
- 'Add note' for each selection

# Output Format

```markdown
## Context for Brenner Loop

### From §42: The choice of organism
> The choice of the experimental object remains one of the most important things...

### From §87: Abundance trick
> If you can find a protein that dominates...

### User note
[Additional context or question]
```

# Persistence

- Store selections in localStorage
- Clear on 'Start Session' or manual clear

# Integration

- Works within document viewer
- Selections persist across page navigations
- Basket accessible from any page

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Text selection triggers popover
- [ ] Selections added to basket
- [ ] Basket shows all selections with metadata
- [ ] Reordering works
- [ ] Export generates correct format
- [ ] Persists across navigation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:15:35.813553979-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T20:15:59.636868573-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T19:29:11.486732227-05:00","close_reason":"Superseded by brenner_bot-5so.4.2.2 + brenner_bot-5so.2.4.1","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0s7","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-v2e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:16:25.083902194-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-0xzg","title":"Task: Coverage Threshold Enforcement (80%)","description":"# Goal
Raise test coverage thresholds to 80% and enforce per-file minimum coverage.

# Current State
```typescript
// vitest.config.ts (current)
coverage: {
  thresholds: {
    lines: 50,
    functions: 50,
    branches: 50,
    statements: 50,
  }
}
```

# Target State
```typescript
// vitest.config.ts (target)
coverage: {
  provider: 'v8',
  reporter: ['text', 'lcov', 'html', 'json'],
  reportsDirectory: './coverage',
  include: ['src/**/*.ts', 'src/**/*.tsx'],
  exclude: [
    'src/**/*.test.ts',
    'src/**/*.test.tsx',
    'src/__fixtures__/**',
    'src/types/**',
    '**/*.d.ts',
  ],
  thresholds: {
    // Global thresholds
    lines: 80,
    functions: 80,
    branches: 75,
    statements: 80,
    
    // Per-file enforcement
    perFile: true,
    
    // Critical files with higher thresholds
    'src/lib/artifact-merge.ts': {
      lines: 90,
      functions: 90,
      branches: 85,
    },
    'src/lib/delta-parser.ts': {
      lines: 90,
      functions: 90,
      branches: 85,
    },
  },
  
  // Fail CI on threshold violation
  skipFull: false,
  
  // Check for uncovered lines
  checkCoverage: true,
}
```

# CI Integration
Update GitHub Actions workflow:
```yaml
# .github/workflows/test.yml
- name: Run tests with coverage
  run: bun test --coverage
  
- name: Check coverage thresholds
  run: |
    # Parse coverage report and fail if below threshold
    bun run coverage:check
    
- name: Upload coverage to Codecov
  uses: codecov/codecov-action@v3
  with:
    files: ./apps/web/coverage/lcov.info
    fail_ci_if_error: true
```

# Coverage Badge
Add to README:
```markdown
[![Coverage](https://codecov.io/gh/user/brenner_bot/branch/main/graph/badge.svg)](https://codecov.io/gh/user/brenner_bot)
```

# Gradual Rollout Plan
1. Week 1: Set global to 60%, add perFile
2. Week 2: Raise to 70%, identify low-coverage files
3. Week 3: Raise to 80%, enforce on all files
4. Ongoing: Add per-file exceptions only with justification

# Coverage Exclusion Guidelines
Exclude from coverage (with comment justification):
- Type definitions (*.d.ts)
- Test files and fixtures
- Generated code
- Configuration files
- Development-only code

DO NOT exclude:
- Complex business logic
- Error handling paths
- Edge cases

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Global threshold at 80% lines
- [ ] Per-file enforcement enabled
- [ ] Critical files at 90%
- [ ] CI fails on threshold violation
- [ ] Coverage badge in README
- [ ] Coverage report uploaded to Codecov
- [ ] Exclusion list documented","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:54:32.108038217-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T13:53:40.25802117-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T13:53:40.25802117-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","labels":["testing infrastructure coverage ci"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0xzg","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-0meb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:56:20.596496889-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0xzg","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-ponv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:56:20.62687603-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0xzg","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-4ep3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:37:16.960318921-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-10m0","title":"Kickoff wiring: operatorSelection through web+CLI role-separated","description":"# Goal\nEnsure operatorSelection (per role) is carried through all role-separated kickoff paths and rendered in kickoff messages.\n\n# Deliverables\n- Extend KickoffConfig to accept operatorSelection.\n- Web API route: pass operatorSelection into composeKickoffMessages for rosterMode=role_separated.\n- CLI session/cockpit kickoff: accept operatorSelection (or default) and include in kickoff messages.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:25:25.875228527-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:50:49.404906126-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T15:50:49.404906126-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-10m0","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-l9ce","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:25:25.880175537-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
